title: Pure Simulation Core, Effect Adapters, Events, and Dependency Direction · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers pure simulation core, effect adapters, events, and dependency direction into a changed testable game core and adapter architecture. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of testable game core and adapter architecture with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical.
Keep display, events, devices, audio, assets, persistence, clock, logging, and packaging behind thin owned adapters.
Emit immutable collision, score, life, wave, cue, and transition records and consume them in explicit order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pure Simulation
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 9970 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pure Simulation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pure Simulation, keep Pure Simulation connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Audit the initial evidence packet · Lab Architecture Evidence
steps.instruction:

Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.text: A lab Asteroids team handles case 8204 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Architecture Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-architecture-evidence-bnwknt" that uses case 1193 to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Architecture Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Architecture Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Architecture Evidence, keep Architecture Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-architecture-evidence-bnwknt
# Competency: asteroids-architecture-evidence.
# Case 1193: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_architecture_evidence_bnwknt(pure_systems: int = 8, effect_adapters: int = 4, hidden_globals: int = 0, dependency_cycles: int = 0, immutable_events: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_bnwknt = "bnwknt-tnkwnb-1193"
    assert evidence_variant_bnwknt
    evidence_axes_bnwknt = (
        "playerbnwknt",
        "seed1193",
        "inputtnkwnb",
        "framebnwknt1193",
        "state1193bnwknt",
        "playtesttnkwnb1193",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_bnwknt)) == 6
    if pure_systems < 4 or effect_adapters < 1:
        return False, pure_systems, "architecture-shape"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if dependency_cycles > 0:
        return False, dependency_cycles, "dependency-cycle"
    if not immutable_events:
        return False, pure_systems, "mutable-events"
    assert pure_systems > effect_adapters
    return True, pure_systems, "testable-game-core"
